note: 7 new leads across 4 pipelines (ai-consulting, pr-marketing, media, fund-formation). AI-consulting dominates today: Wonderful ($150M Series B at $2B valuation from Insight Partners — scaling 300→900 people across 30 countries), Solidroad ($25M Series A April 16 from Hedosophia — AI-native QA/coaching for customer teams, Dublin/SF), and Parasail ($32M Series A April 15 from Touring/Kindred — AI inference supercloud processing 500B tokens/day) all freshly raised with explicit hiring + go-to-market buildouts. PR/marketing pair: Joyful Health ($17M Series A April 16 from CRV — healthcare AI financial ops, $1.4B processed) and ScreenPoint Medical ($16M April 17 from Insight + Siemens Healthineers — breast cancer AI in 30+ countries with Lancet-published MASAI trial results). Media pipeline gets a high-signal target: Nas.com ($27M Series A April 16 from Khosla — creator Nuseir Yassin's solopreneur AI platform, 3.5M members, public-facing founder at 70M followers). Fund-formation lead: Shiprock Capital Management readying new debt-focused hedge fund launch with ex-Deutsche Bank EM debt head Alex Williamson (emerging manager pattern — existing distressed/EM fund from 2023, now launching new strategy). Neon DB unreachable from sandbox — run `node scripts/import-2026-04-21.cjs` locally to import. Deduplicated against 60+ existing companies from prior scrapes (Wealth.com, Summize, Dolomite, Eyre Street, Air Street, Fedaia, Ferghana, OpenArc, Orbital, depthfirst, Tazapay, Round Treasury, Spektr, Complyance, FINTECH.TV, Sigla Capital, Awani Capital, Eudia, Lawhive, Littlefish, Haast, Delve, Gizmo, Goldenpeak, Shepherd, InsightFinder, Gumloop, Checkbox, Felix, Aqualis Partners, Sycamore Tree, 5c(c) Capital).

leads.name: Wonderful
leads.industry: AI Agents / Customer Service Automation / Enterprise AI
leads.size: ~300 employees scaling to 900; operates in 30+ countries; serves telecom, finance, healthcare, manufacturing
leads.fundingStage: Series B ($150M, March 12, 2026) at $2B valuation — led by Insight Partners with Index Ventures, IVP, Bessemer, Vine Ventures (total raised $284M in 13 months)
leads.notes: Raised $150M Series B at $2B valuation on March 12, 2026 — only 4 months after $100M Series A and 13 months after founding. Explicitly tripling headcount from 300 to 900 to scale local deployment teams across 30+ countries. Serves telecom, finance, healthcare, manufacturing — classic enterprise AI consulting ICP. CEO is public-facing. Insight Partners lead signals enterprise software sales DNA. Primary angle: partner on enterprise AI-agent deployments, change management, and localization strategy for US market entry. Secondary angle: pr-marketing amplification of the hypergrowth narrative (13 months to $2B is a headline any PR shop would kill for).

leads.name: Solidroad
leads.industry: AI Quality Assurance / Customer Support Coaching / Contact Center Enablement
leads.size: ~20 employees (Dublin + SF); customers include Ryanair, Crypto.com, Oura, PartnerHero, Tech Mahindra
leads.fundingStage: Series A ($25M, April 16, 2026) — led by Hedosophia with First Round Capital, Y Combinator, Sony Innovation Fund (total raised $31.5M)
leads.source: TechCrunch / Tech.eu / The Next Web / VentureBeat / Silicon Republic (April 16, 2026)
leads.notes: $25M Series A led by Hedosophia on April 16, 2026 — 5 days ago. YC alum founded by ex-Intercom operators Mark Hughes & Patrick Finlay (2023). Positioned as AI-native QA that covers 100% of customer interactions (vs. sampling) AND coaches agents — that bridge positioning plays well in the 'AI augments rather than replaces' narrative enterprise buyers want. Explicitly hiring into the Series A. Small team (20 people) means they need external go-to-market and deployment muscle. Customer list of Ryanair, Crypto.com, Oura is credible. Perfect fit for AI-consulting partner role — especially for enterprises already deploying Intercom/Zendesk AI agents who need quality/compliance layer.

leads.name: Parasail
leads.industry: AI Infrastructure / Inference Cloud / GPU Supercloud
leads.size: Growth stage; processes 500B+ tokens/day; 30% MoM revenue growth; customers include Elicit, mem0, Gravity, Kotoba, Venice
leads.fundingStage: Series A ($32M, April 15, 2026) — co-led by Touring Capital and Kindred Ventures, with Samsung NEXT, Flume Ventures, Banyan Ventures (total raised $42M)
leads.notes: $32M Series A on April 15, 2026 co-led by Touring Capital + Kindred. AI supercloud for deploying + scaling AI agents — pay-per-token GPU capacity with no long-term contracts. Processes 500B tokens/day, 30% MoM revenue growth since April 2025 launch. Same investor (Touring Capital) backed Checkbox in January — institutional signal overlap. Parasail is arguably a channel partner for AI-consulting practice more than a direct client: firms building AI agents need compute + consulting bundled. Secondary angle as pr-marketing target: fresh raise + 'tokenmaxxing' narrative is fresh enough for earned-media amplification. Would benefit from enterprise GTM positioning help as they move upmarket.

leads.name: Joyful Health
leads.industry: HealthTech / AI Revenue Cycle Management / Financial Operations for Providers
leads.size: Growth stage; processed $1.4B+ in healthcare transactions; 95%+ recovery rate across specialties
leads.fundingStage: Series A ($17M, April 16, 2026) — led by CRV with XYZ Venture Capital, Designer Fund, Inflect Capital (Vituity), Go Global Ventures (total raised $22M)
leads.source: PR Newswire / HIT Consultant / Axios Pro / FinSMEs (April 16, 2026)
leads.notes: $17M Series A led by CRV on April 16, 2026 — 5 days ago. AI-powered financial infrastructure for healthcare providers tackling $125B in lost healthcare revenue. CEO Eliana Berger + CTO Warren Green. 95%+ recovery rate and $1.4B+ processed is a strong, quantifiable PR story. Vituity (largest physician-owned partnership in US) backed via Inflect Capital — signals real industry traction. Post-raise PR amplification target: healthcare + fintech + AI is a triple-narrative fit. Use cases: launch PR, founder profiles, thought-leadership on the $125B lost revenue problem. Also borderline ai-consulting fit for healthcare enterprise deployments.

leads.name: ScreenPoint Medical
leads.industry: MedTech / AI Breast Cancer Detection / Diagnostic Imaging
leads.size: Deployed in 30+ countries; processed 12M+ mammograms; global partnerships with Siemens Healthineers
leads.fundingStage: Growth round ($16M, April 17, 2026) — $14M from Insight Partners + Siemens Healthineers, $2M in non-dilutive research grants (total raised $49M)
leads.source: PR Newswire / Insight Partners / Medical Device Network / Femtech Insider (April 17, 2026)
leads.notes: $16M round on April 17, 2026 from existing investors Insight Partners + Siemens Healthineers. Coincident with Lancet-published MASAI randomised controlled trial showing 12% reduction in interval cancers via Transpara. Rare combination: fresh capital + fresh peer-reviewed clinical evidence = premium PR-amplification window. Already in 30+ countries with 12M+ mammograms processed. US market expansion is a named use-of-funds priority. European firm needing US-focused PR/communications strategy + media training on the clinical data story. High credibility, high narrative leverage. Secondary read: founder-friendly and willing to invest in narrative given the MASAI milestone.

leads.name: Nas.com
leads.industry: Creator Economy / AI Tools for Solopreneurs / Community & Courses Platform
leads.size: 3.5M+ members in 150+ countries; 350K+ businesses hosted; 20K paying monthly; $1M→$8M ARR in 2025
leads.fundingStage: Series A ($27M, April 16, 2026) — led by Khosla Ventures with 500 Global, angel investors including Shuo Wang (Deel), Stanley Tang (DoorDash), Scott Adelson (Houlihan Lokey), Tim Ferriss
leads.source: PR Newswire / Yahoo Finance / The AI Journal / Ventureburn (April 16, 2026)
leads.notes: $27M Series A led by Khosla Ventures on April 16, 2026. Founded by Nuseir Yassin (Nas Daily, 70M followers, 30B video views, 64 countries). 5x revenue growth in 2025 ($1M→$8M ARR). Archetype media target: public-facing founder with massive organic distribution + fresh institutional capital + B2C-to-solopreneur narrative. Potential uses: branded podcast partnership targeting solopreneur/creator audience, cross-promo with existing pay-to-play media pipeline (FINTECH.TV analog), interview-format media where Nas himself is the draw. Less a traditional PR client, more a credibility-and-distribution partnership. Angels list is a who's who of founder-led distribution.

leads.name: Shiprock Capital Management
leads.industry: Hedge Fund / Distressed Credit / Emerging Markets Debt
leads.size: Boutique emerging manager; first fund launched January 2023 (Global Distressed and Special Situations)
leads.fundingStage: New debt-focused hedge fund planning 2026 launch — existing fund +6% YTD through Nov, +32.4% launch year, +34.5% in 2024
leads.source: Hedgeweek / Bloomberg / Alternative Fund Insight (December 2025 – January 2026)
leads.notes: Emerging London hedge fund (launched 2023) readying new debt-focused strategy with recent senior hire: Alex Williamson, ex-head of EM debt at Deutsche Bank. Existing fund has strong track record (+32.4% / +34.5% / +6% YTD Nov 2025), which de-risks the new-strategy raise. Archetype fund-formation target: emerging manager launching Fund II or separate vehicle with new PM, needs LPA + fund docs + compliance setup + possibly new offshore vehicle for EM-focused strategy. UK/offshore jurisdiction complexity = legal work opportunity. Channel: warm intro via prime broker or admin would be ideal; cold requires careful positioning (London alt market is referral-heavy). Hired CRO Cura in January 2026 from Converium — team building actively.

contacts.name: Alex Williamson
contacts.title: Head of Debt / PM — New Strategy (ex-Deutsche Bank EM debt head)
